details: Current evidence blocks covered by the readiness index.
u marginal and x-local stationarity lines meet numeric proof thresholds but still need proof-level method/source binding.
Expanded one-wall/wall-pileup refinement meets numeric proof-threshold lines but remains candidate-only and not proof registered.
Near-boundary expected-band method is bound as candidate evidence using area(radius+band) differences; external review should assess method and threshold, not proof/pass.
Worst current substep requirement before any runtime policy.
Runtime/substep policy classes are design-bound, including prohibitive cost handling. User ledger authorizes the path; runtime output still requires implementation tests and an execution packet.

Legacy pre-user-authorization preflight binds a GitHub-visible candidate commit and fail-closed manual-ledger placeholder; it is superseded by user_authorization_ledger for path authorization only and still does not register proof/pass or runtime output.
User authorization ledger accepts proof-registration, runtime/substep, solver, and wet branch paths; result-promotion guards still require evidence packets.
